text:
National Institute of Technology Calicut 
Dept. of Electronics and Communication Engineering 
EC3011 WIRELESS COMMUNICATION 
Exercise problems #1(09.01.2026) 
(Make suitable assumptions for missing data, if any) 
1. Consider a wireless communications system with the following parameters:  transmitted power: +20 
dBm; minimum required receive d power: −100 dBm ; transmitting antenna gain: 10 dB ; receiving 
antenna gain: 0 dB; carrier frequency: 1 GHz.

antenna gain: 0 dB; carrier frequency: 1 GHz. 
a. What is the range (the distance over which the wireless system will work) in free space?  
b.  Assume the signal propagates over an ideal flat surface w ith reflection coefficient of −1.  
Compute the range for a transmitting antenna height of ℎ𝑡 = 7.5 m and a receiving antenna  
height of ℎ𝑟 = 1.8 m, and compare with part a. 
2. If the received power at a reference distance d0 = 1 km is 1 micro -Watt, find the received power at

distance d = 10 km for (i) free space model and (ii) model with path loss exponent 3.  
3. Consider the two ray ground reflected model for path loss calculation. It was shown that t he received 
power at a distance  d from the tran smitter has log -normal probability distribution under large scale 
fading. Find the probability that the received power at a distance, d will fall below the minimum value 
required (Pmin) to meet a certain QoS criterion.

required (Pmin) to meet a certain QoS criterion.  
4. Find the critical distance for the two path ground-reflected model for a large macro -cell in a 
suburban area with base station mounted on a tower or building with  antenna heights ℎ𝑡 =20 meters; 
ℎ𝑟=3 meters; and carrier frequency f=2GHz. 
5. Consider the combined path loss -shadow fading model. A transmitter provides 15 Watts to an 
antenna having gain 12  dB. The receiver antenna has a gain of 3  dB and the receiver bandwidth is 30

kHz. Determine the maximum separation between transmitter and receiver that will ensure a 
minimum received pow er of -101 dBm for 95% of the time  (i.e., outage probability is equal to 5%). 
Assume: Path loss exponent = 4; Carrier frequency = 1800 MHz; Standard deviation of log normal 
shadowing = 8 dB; and 
0d  = 1 km. 
6. Consider the simplified path l oss model discussed in the class.  Assume that an average power equal

to -100dBm is to be maintained at a cell boundary. Find the cell radius. Given that the path loss 
constant K (path gain at reference distance of 
0d  = 1 km) is 18 dB below the corresponding K value 
for free-space. 
7. A transmitter operates at  1.8 GHz . Compare the path loss at a distance of  1 km  for the following 
environments using the log-distance path loss model. Assume the reference distance  d₀ = 100 m and

environments using the log-distance path loss model. Assume the reference distance  d₀ = 100 m and 
path loss at d₀ is 70 dB. (i) Free space (n = 2), (ii) Suburban area (n = 2.7) and (iii) Urban area (n = 
3.5). 
8. Justify the use of Log-normal PDF for representing the large scale fading  effects in wireless channels. 
Let Ψ𝑑𝐵 has Normal (i.e, Gaussian) PDF with mean μ and variance σ2 (all quantities in dB). Define 
Ψ = 10Ψ𝑑𝐵 10⁄ . Find the PDF of Ψ.

Ψ = 10Ψ𝑑𝐵 10⁄ . Find the PDF of Ψ.  
9. Consider a cellular system operating at 900 MHz , where propagation foll ows free space path loss 
with variations about  this path from log -normal shad owing with σ = 6 dB. Suppose that for 
acceptable voice quality, a signal-to-noise power ratio of 15 dB  is required at the mobile. Assume the 
base station transmits at 1 W and its a ntenna has a 3 dB gain.  There is no antenna gain at the mobile

and the receiver noise in the bandwidth of interest is -40 dBm. Find the maximum cell size so that a 
mobile on the cell boundary will have acceptable voice quality  90% of the time. 
10. A base station radiates at a transmit power of 50W. Let the carrier frequency, fc, be 900MHz. Let d0 
be 100m. Assume free space path loss model between transmitter  and d0, and combined path loss 
plus log-normal shadowing model for distances greater than d0. Assume path loss exponent, n, of 3

plus log-normal shadowing model for distances greater than d0. Assume path loss exponent, n, of 3 
and of 𝜎=5dB for log-normal shadowing model. Find the following: 
a. The received power (in dBm) at 100m. 
b. The mean path loss (in dB) from transmitter at 500m. 
c. The mean received power (in dBm) at 500m.  
d. The probability that the received power at d=500m is greater than or equal to 0.01mW.
